description: You are an agricultural water resource analyst mapping the Nile River corridor between Aswan and Luxor, Egypt. Using Google Earth Pro: (1) Import /home/ga/Documents/nile_survey_points.csv containing 12 survey stations along the Nile. Map the 'latitude' column to Latitude, 'longitude' column to Longitude, and 'name' column to Name. (2) Create a folder named 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey'. (3) Inside the folder, draw a path named 'Nile Main Channel' tracing the river from Aswan High Dam (24.0286N, 32.8780E) downstream to Luxor Temple (25.6995N, 32.6390E) with at least 25 waypoints closely following the river's visible course, styled blue with width 3. (4) Inside the same folder, draw two polygons outlining visible irrigated farmland against the desert, each with at least 10 vertices closely tracing the cultivation boundary: 'East Bank Agriculture' near Esna (green, 50% transparent) and 'West Bank Agriculture' near Kom Ombo (green, 50% transparent). (5) Tilt the view to approximately 60 degrees and save a 3D terrain screenshot to ~/Documents/nile_survey_3d.png. (6) Export the complete folder as KMZ to ~/Documents/nile_agricultural_survey.kmz.
